metadata.title: A Two-Second Flash Brighter Than All Galaxies — Because It Was a Beam
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = true energy / jet geometry / beaming factor; CRIMSON = isotropic assumption / apparent energy / physical impossibility
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no GRB afterglow, no Ordovician extinction detail, no ozone depletion mechanism. Wonder register.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B11: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: A pinprick of gamma rays flickers for two seconds and vanishes. For that instant, it appears to outshine every star in every galaxy combined. The energy budget is physically impossible — unless we have been making a geometric mistake.

card.copy: A Two-Second Flash Brighter Than All Galaxies — Because It Was a Beam

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A pinprick of gamma rays flickers for two seconds and vanishes. For that instant, it appears to outshine every star in every galaxy combined. The energy budget

beats.narration_text: Gamma-ray bursts: sudden flares from billions of light-years away. Computing the energy by assuming isotropic emission — radiation spreading equally in all directions — gives a number that exceeds what any physical process can produce in two seconds.

production_viz.label: Sphere of radiation spreading isotropically from GRB source; 10^47 J label in CRIMSON showing impossibility

beats.narration_text: If the isotropic energy budget is impossible, what geometric assumption turns an impossible number into a physically plausible one?

card.copy: GRB: 2 seconds, apparently brighter than all galaxies. Isotropic energy: impossible. What geometry saves the physics?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: If the isotropic energy budget is impossible, what geometric assumption turns an impossible number into a physically plausible one?

beats.narration_text: The fix: the radiation is not isotropic. It is a relativistic jet — beaming out in a narrow cone of perhaps one to five degrees. The apparent energy assumes radiation spread over all 4π steradians. A jet fills only a tiny fraction of that.

production_viz.label: Sphere morphing into narrow jet cone; 4π steradians vs tiny cone fraction; TEAL for jet, CRIMSON for discarded isotropic assumption

beats.narration_text: True energy equals apparent energy times the fraction of the sphere covered by the jet: 1 minus cosine theta. For theta equal 2 degrees: that factor is about 6 times 10 to the minus 4.

production_viz.label: E_true = E_apparent × (1 - cos θ) equation; θ=2° annotated; factor 6×10^-4 highlighted in TEAL

beats.narration_text: Apparent isotropic energy: roughly 10 to the 47 joules — exceeding the Sun's entire lifetime output. Apply the jet factor: true energy is around 6 times 10 to the 43 joules — comparable to what a collapsing star or neutron star merger can actually release.

production_viz.label: Energy comparison: 10^47 J (CRIMSON, impossible) → ×6×10^-4 → 6×10^43 J (TEAL, plausible); stellar collapse reference

beats.narration_text: The jet forms when a black hole plus accretion disk accelerates material to near-light speed along the rotation axis. The jet punches through the collapsing stellar envelope and beams energy outward. We only see it if the beam points at us.

production_viz.label: Black hole + accretion disk diagram with twin jets emerging along rotation axis; TEAL jets beaming outward

beats.narration_text: This explains both the extreme brightness — we're looking straight down the jet — and the rarity. Most gamma-ray bursts are aimed elsewhere. We only see those pointed almost exactly at Earth.

production_viz.label: Multiple GRBs with jets pointing in random directions; Earth only aligned with one; TEAL for visible GRB aimed at us

beats.narration_text: Nearby GRBs within a few thousand light-years, aimed at Earth, could strip the ozone layer. The evidence suggests this has happened — the Ordovician extinction is one candidate.

production_viz.label: Close GRB within a few kly aimed at Earth; ozone layer impact indicated; CRIMSON for threat

beats.narration_text: Isotropic assumption: apparent energy 10 to the 47 joules — impossible. Jet half-angle theta approximately 2 degrees: factor 1 minus cosine theta approximately 6 times 10 to the minus 4. True energy approximately 6 times 10 to the 43 joules — physical. GRBs are beamed. We only see ones aimed at us.

card.copy: GRB apparent: 10^47 J (impossible). Jet θ ≈ 2°: factor ≈ 6×10^-4. True energy ≈ 6×10^43 J — plausible. Beaming = brightness illusion. We only see GRBs aimed at Earth.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Isotropic assumption: apparent energy 10 to the 47 joules — impossible. Jet half-angle theta approximately 2 degrees: factor 1 minus cosine theta approximately

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
